mechanism: Inhibits CYP2C9 metabolism of warfarin increasing INR
recommendation: Monitor INR closely; limit paracetamol to <2g/day

mechanism: Dual anticoagulant/antiplatelet effect increases bleeding risk
recommendation: Avoid combination unless cardiology-directed; monitor for bleeding signs

mechanism: CYP2C9 inhibition increases warfarin effect
recommendation: Reduce warfarin dose 25-50%; check INR day 3-5

mechanism: Strong CYP2C9 inhibition dramatically increases INR
recommendation: Reduce warfarin dose 50%; daily INR monitoring

mechanism: NSAIDs may reduce renal function affecting metformin clearance
recommendation: Monitor renal function; consider paracetamol alternative

mechanism: Increased risk of lactic acidosis
recommendation: Advise moderate alcohol only; monitor lactate if symptomatic

mechanism: Both increase serum potassium risk of hyperkalemia
recommendation: Monitor K+ levels; avoid K+ supplements unless hypokalemic

mechanism: Dual potassium-sparing effect hyperkalemia risk
recommendation: Check K+ within 1 week of starting; monitor regularly

mechanism: Amlodipine increases simvastatin exposure via CYP3A4
recommendation: Limit simvastatin to 20mg/day with amlodipine

mechanism: Amiodarone increases digoxin levels 70-100%
recommendation: Halve digoxin dose when starting amiodarone; monitor levels

mechanism: Omeprazole inhibits CYP2C19 reducing clopidogrel activation
recommendation: Switch to pantoprazole or rabeprazole

mechanism: Both are folate antagonists; trimethoprim reduces methotrexate clearance
recommendation: Avoid combination; if unavoidable increase folinic acid rescue

mechanism: Ciprofloxacin inhibits CYP1A2 increasing theophylline levels
recommendation: Monitor theophylline level; consider dose reduction 30-50%

mechanism: NSAIDs reduce lithium clearance causing toxicity
recommendation: Monitor lithium level; use paracetamol instead

mechanism: Serotonin syndrome risk from dual serotonergic effect
recommendation: Avoid combination; use alternative analgesic

mechanism: Beta-blockers mask hypoglycemia symptoms
recommendation: Educate patient on alternative hypoglycemia signs; monitor glucose

mechanism: Amoxicillin reduces renal tubular secretion of methotrexate
recommendation: Monitor methotrexate levels and renal function

mechanism: CYP3A4 induction reduces contraceptive efficacy
recommendation: Use higher-dose OCP or alternative contraception

mechanism: Complex bidirectional interaction affecting both drug levels
recommendation: Monitor levels of both drugs; clinical signs

mechanism: Potent CYP3A4 inducer eliminates contraceptive efficacy
recommendation: Use non-hormonal contraception during and 28 days after rifampicin
